criteria.label: State the canonical deep learning definition and its two defining characteristics.
criteria.example: Write one sentence: DL = ML with multi-layer neural nets learning representations end-to-end; list neural depth and representation learning as the two pillars.

criteria.label: Place DL correctly in the AI → ML → DL → Gen AI hierarchy.
criteria.example: Draw or describe nesting: AI contains ML contains DL; note Gen AI as a largely DL-powered application layer focused on generation.

criteria.label: Contrast deep learning with classical ML on features and data needs.
criteria.example: Give a tabular vs image scenario: CSV with 300 rows → boosting with engineered stats; 100k labeled photos → CNN learning filters from pixels.

criteria.label: Identify when depth and representation learning help versus when a baseline suffices.
criteria.example: Propose a validation plan: train logistic regression and a two-hidden-layer MLP on the same split; adopt DL only if lift justifies compute and labeling cost.
